prompt:
You are a blind ceiling-quality judge. You receive one task and two anonymized candidate patches. Do not infer or mention model, arm, attempt, harness, source run, or author identity; judge only the task text and diffs.

Task text:
Add a run-budget feature to the `schedule` library (this repository).

Implement `Job.max_runs(n)`:

- Chainable setter that returns the Job instance, usable anywhere in the
  fluent chain, e.g. `schedule.every(5).seconds.max_runs(3).do(job_fn)` or
  `schedule.every().max_runs(3).seconds.do(job_fn)`.
- The budget counts actual executions of the job function. After the job
  has executed n times in total, the job removes itself from its scheduler
  exactly as a job returning `CancelJob` does. The nth execution itself
  still runs normally; an (n+1)-th execution must never happen.
- Applies to every execution path that runs jobs through the scheduler
  (`run_pending()` and `run_all()` included).
- Interaction with `.until(deadline)`: whichever limit is reached first
  cancels the job (first-limit-wins). The existing `.until()` semantics
  must not regress.
- After a job exhausts its budget, `Scheduler.next_run` / `idle_seconds`
  must reflect its removal.
- Validation: n must be an integer >= 1; any other value raises
  ValueError at `.max_runs(...)` call time. Calling `.max_runs()` more
  than once on the same job: the last call wins.
- Budgets are per-job: multiple jobs with different budgets must not
  interfere with each other.
- Document the new API wherever this project documents comparable Job
  methods, and keep the implementation idiomatic for this codebase.

Deliver production-quality code: the full existing test suite must keep
passing, and the new behavior must be covered by tests you add.

diff --git a/docs/examples.rst b/docs/examples.rst

--- a/docs/examples.rst

@@ -244,6 +244,21 @@ Run a job until a certain time
 
 The ``until`` method sets the jobs deadline. The job will not run after the deadline.
 
+Limit the number of runs
+~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
+
+Use ``max_runs`` to remove a job after it has run a fixed number of times.
+
+.. code-block:: python
+
+    import schedule
+
+    def job():
+        print('Boo')
+
+    # Run the job three times, then remove it.
+    schedule.every().hour.max_runs(3).do(job)
+
 Time until the next execution
 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 Use ``schedule.idle_seconds()`` to get the number of seconds until the next job is scheduled to run.

+    def test_max_runs(self):
+        mock_job = make_mock_job()
+
+        job = every().max_runs(3)

+        assert job.do(mock_job) is job
+        job = every().seconds
+        assert job.max_runs(3) is job

+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 10, 0, 2):
+            schedule.run_pending()
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 10, 0, 3):
+            schedule.run_pending()
+            assert pending_job.call_count == 2
+            assert schedule.next_run() is None
+            assert schedule.idle_seconds() is None
+
+        run_all_job = make_mock_job()
+        every().second.max_runs(2).do(run_all_job)
+        schedule.run_all()
+        schedule.run_all()
+        schedule.run_all()
+        assert run_all_job.call_count == 2
+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0):
+            deadline_first_job = (
+                every().second.max_runs(3).until(datetime.time(10, 0, 2)).do(mock_job)
+            )
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 10, 0, 1):
+            schedule.run_pending()
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 10, 0, 3):
+            schedule.run_pending()
+            assert mock_job.call_count == 1
+            assert deadline_first_job._runs_remaining == 2
+            assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 10, 0, 0):
+            every().second.max_runs(1).until(datetime.time(10, 0, 10)).do(mock_job)
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 10, 0, 1):
+            schedule.run_pending()
+            assert mock_job.call_count == 2
+            assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+        last_call_wins_job = make_mock_job()
+        every().second.max_runs(1).max_runs(3).do(last_call_wins_job)
+        schedule.run_all()
+        schedule.run_all()
+        schedule.run_all()
+        assert last_call_wins_job.call_count == 3
+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+        limited_job = make_mock_job()
+        unlimited_job = make_mock_job()
+        every().second.max_runs(1).do(limited_job)
+        unlimited = every().second.do(unlimited_job)
+        schedule.run_all()
+        schedule.run_all()
+        assert limited_job.call_count == 1
+        assert unlimited_job.call_count == 2
+        assert schedule.jobs == [unlimited]
+
     def test_weekday_at_todady(self):
         mock_job = make_mock_job()

--- a/docs/examples.rst
+++ b/docs/examples.rst
@@ -244,6 +244,26 @@ Run a job until a certain time
 
 The ``until`` method sets the jobs deadline. The job will not run after the deadline.
 
+
+Run a job a limited number of times
+~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
+
+Use ``max_runs`` to automatically remove a job after a fixed number of executions.
+
+.. code-block:: python
+
+    import schedule
+
+    def job():
+        print('Boo')
+
+    # Run job at most three times.
+    schedule.every(5).seconds.max_runs(3).do(job)
+
+The final allowed execution runs normally before the job is removed. ``max_runs`` can
+be combined with ``until``; the job is removed when either limit is reached first.
+
+
 Time until the next execution
 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 Use ``schedule.idle_seconds()`` to get the number of seconds until the next job is scheduled to run.

+    def test_max_runs(self):
+        mock_job = make_mock_job()
+
+        job = every(5).max_runs(3).seconds.do(mock_job)
+        assert job is schedule.jobs[0]
+
+        schedule.run_all()
+        schedule.run_all()

+
+        schedule.run_all()

+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+        schedule.run_all()
+        assert mock_job.call_count == 3
+
+    def test_max_runs_applies_to_run_pending(self):
+        mock_job = make_mock_job()
+
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 10):
+            every().second.max_runs(2).do(mock_job)
+            with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 11):
+                schedule.run_pending()
+            with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 12):
+                schedule.run_pending()
+            with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 13):
+                schedule.run_pending()
+
+        assert mock_job.call_count == 2
+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+    def test_max_runs_counts_job_functions_that_raise(self):
+        def failing_job():
+            raise RuntimeError
+
+        every().second.max_runs(1).do(failing_job)
+        with self.assertRaises(RuntimeError):
+            schedule.run_all()
+
+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+    def test_max_runs_validation_and_replacement(self):
+        job = every()
+        for invalid_value in (0, -1, 1.0, "1", None, True):
+            with self.assertRaises(ValueError):
+                job.max_runs(invalid_value)
+
+        mock_job = make_mock_job()
+        every().max_runs(1).second.max_runs(2).do(mock_job)
+        schedule.run_all()
+        schedule.run_all()
+
+        assert mock_job.call_count == 2
+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+    def test_max_runs_are_per_job_and_update_scheduler_state(self):
+        first_job = make_mock_job("first_job")
+        second_job = make_mock_job("second_job")
+
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 10):
+            every().hour.max_runs(1).do(first_job)
+            every().hour.max_runs(2).do(second_job)
+            assert schedule.idle_seconds() == 60 * 60
+
+            schedule.run_all()
+            assert first_job.call_count == 1
+            assert second_job.call_count == 1
+            assert len(schedule.jobs) == 1
+            assert schedule.idle_seconds() == 60 * 60
+
+            schedule.run_all()
+            assert second_job.call_count == 2
+            assert schedule.next_run() is None
+            assert schedule.idle_seconds() is None
+
+    def test_max_runs_and_until_cancel_when_the_first_limit_is_reached(self):
+        deadline_limited_job = make_mock_job("deadline_limited_job")
+
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 10):
+            every(5).seconds.max_runs(3).until("11:35:20").do(deadline_limited_job)
+            with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 15):
+                schedule.run_pending()
+            with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 20):
+                schedule.run_pending()
+
+        assert deadline_limited_job.call_count == 2
+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
+        budget_limited_job = make_mock_job("budget_limited_job")
+        with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 10):
+            every(5).seconds.max_runs(2).until("11:36:00").do(budget_limited_job)
+            with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 15):
+                schedule.run_pending()
+            with mock_datetime(2020, 1, 1, 11, 35, 20):
+                schedule.run_pending()
+
+        assert budget_limited_job.call_count == 2
+        assert schedule.jobs == []
+
     def test_weekday_at_todady(self):
         mock_job = make_mock_job()

strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 models the requirement as `_max_runs` plus total `_run_count`, while P1 resets `_runs_remaining` on every call and therefore treats a later `.max_runs(n)` as additional remaining executions.
P2 rejects `True` and counts/removes a budget-exhausted job even when `job_func` raises, whereas P1 accepts booleans and only decrements after a successful return.
P2 immediately cancels an already-scheduled job when a replacement limit is at or below its existing `_run_count`, preserving the total-execution budget across later `.max_runs()` calls.
P2 documents the API in `HISTORY.rst`, `README.rst`, and examples, and its method docstring explicitly states replacement semantics and `ValueError` validation.

diff --git a/docs/examples.rst b/docs/examples.rst
index ac8f32a..9817324 100644
--- a/docs/examples.rst
+++ b/docs/examples.rst
@@ -244,6 +244,26 @@ Run a job until a certain time
 
 The ``until`` method sets the jobs deadline. The job will not run after the deadline.
 
+
+Run a job a limited number of times
+~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
+
+Use ``max_runs`` to automatically remove a job after a fixed number of executions.
+
+.. code-block:: python
+
+    import schedule
+
+    def job():
+        print('Boo')
+
+    # Run job at most three times.
+    schedule.every(5).seconds.max_runs(3).do(job)
+
+The final allowed execution runs normally before the job is removed. ``max_runs`` can
+be combined with ``until``; the job is removed when either limit is reached first.
+
+
 Time until the next execution
 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 Use ``schedule.idle_seconds()`` to get the number of seconds until the next job is scheduled to run.
